threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you ask for directions to the nearest Tube station and locals just stare at you like you’ve grown a second head.
Caught the 45 last Saturday; the driver stopped to help a guy who dropped his kebab. We all cheered when he handed him a fresh one from his bag.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw someone pulling a suitcase filled with plants on the U-Bahn. Only in Neukölln would that seem completely normal.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you stumble upon a techno party in an abandoned subway station, complete with a makeshift bar and art installation. Pure magic!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: People from here know to avoid the 49 after 4 PM. The bus drivers look like they’ve given up, and that’s saying something.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today with a guy playing a ukulele. He serenaded us through the rain—only in SF do you get free live music with your commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the plazas are for lounging, but locals actually use them for community meetings and gathering info on the latest neighborhood gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they order coffee – still asking for 'café con leche' when everyone else just says 'café'.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Tube'. In Dalston, it's just 'the train'—and you definitely don't use it after 10 PM.
Caught the N159 last night, and a group of teens were debating if the ghost of Brixton market still haunts the area. Their theories? Wild.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a toddler arguing with their parent over the last piece of baklava at the Turkish market. That’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name all the taco trucks on 24th without missing a beat, but the tourists always ask for that one in the souvenir t-shirt.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni crammed with tourists staring at their phones, totally missing the street performer belting out 'I Will Survive' at the corner.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia’s plazas are just for photos, but they’re actually where locals argue over Barça scores and share home-cooked recipes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they clutch their guidebooks like they're life preservers, even in a tapas bar.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted the local kids setting up a lemonade stand in front of the Kreuzberg Museum, right next to a pop-up tattoo parlor. That’s Neukölln for you.
